title: CDC Laboratory Leadership Service (LLS) Fellowship 2027 Applications Open: Fully Paid Two-Year Public Health Laboratory Leadership Program in the United States
summary: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has officially opened applications for the Laboratory Leadership Service (LLS) Class of 2027, one of the most prestigious public health laboratory leadership programs in the United States. Designed to develop the next generation of laboratory leaders, the fellowship combines advanced scientific training, leadership development, public health service, and hands-on field experience. For doctoral-level laboratory scientists seeking to expand their impact beyond the laboratory bench and become influential leaders in public health, the CDC Laboratory Leadership Service Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to the protection of public health and safety. What is the CDC Laboratory Leadership Service (LLS)? The Laboratory Leadership Service (LLS) is a two-year postdoctoral service-learning fellowship established by the CDC in 2015 to strengthen public health laboratory leadership, laboratory quality management, biosafety practices, and workforce development. The program was developed to create a pipeline of highly skilled laboratory professionals capable of addressing emerging public health challenges while maintaining the highest standards of laboratory excellence, quality, and safety. Unlike traditional research fellowships, LLS combines technical laboratory expertise with real-world public health applications. Fellows receive extensive training while working on critical public health issues affecting communities across the United States. Why the CDC Created the LLS Fellowship Modern public health threats require laboratory professionals who possess more than scientific expertise. Public health laboratories increasingly need leaders capable of managing laboratory systems, responding to emergencies, communicating scientific findings, and overseeing biosafety and quality assurance programs. The CDC created the LLS Fellowship to: Strengthen laboratory leadership capacity. Improve laboratory safety and quality standards. Develop future public health laboratory leaders. Support disease surveillance and outbreak investigations. Enhance emergency preparedness and response capabilities. Promote interdisciplinary collaboration between laboratory scientists and epidemiologists. What Fellows Do During the Program LLS fellows participate in a wide range of activities that provide practical experience in public health laboratory science. During the fellowship, participants: 1. Conduct Public Health Investigations Fellows work on cutting-edge laboratory investigations that support public health decision-making and disease prevention efforts. 2. Support Outbreak Responses Participants contribute to responses involving disease outbreaks, emerging health threats, disasters, and public health emergencies. 3. Gain Leadership Training The fellowship places significant emphasis on leadership development, preparing scientists to lead public health laboratories and programs. 4. Learn Laboratory Quality Management Fellows receive training in: Quality management systems Laboratory operations Quality assurance Continuous improvement strategies 5. Strengthen Biosafety Expertise Participants learn best practices in laboratory biosafety and biosecurity to ensure safe laboratory operations. 6. Develop Communication Skills The program helps fellows communicate scientific findings effectively to policymakers, health professionals, and the public. 7. Build Bioinformatics and Data Analysis Skills Participants gain experience using modern analytical tools to interpret scientific and public health data. Fellowship Training Structure The fellowship begins each July with an intensive month-long orientation and training course in Atlanta, Georgia. Throughout the two-year program, fellows receive: Hands-on laboratory training. One-on-one mentorship from CDC experts. Leadership development opportunities. Emergency response experience. Applied public health investigations. Conference presentations. Networking opportunities with leading public health professionals. Collaborative learning alongside CDC s Epidemic Intelligence Service (EIS) officers. Fellowship Benefits The CDC provides fellows with numerous professional and financial benefits. These include: Federal employment status during the fellowship. Competitive salary equivalent to GS-12 Step 3 federal pay. Locality pay adjustments based on assignment location. Federal health insurance benefits. Retirement benefits. Life insurance coverage. Dental and vision insurance. Annual leave and sick leave. Access to extensive CDC training resources. Career advancement opportunities within public health. Host Site Assignments Selected fellows may be assigned to: CDC headquarters laboratories in Atlanta. CDC laboratories located elsewhere in the United States. State public health laboratories. Territorial public health laboratories. Local public health laboratories. Fellows must be willing to relocate to their assigned location. Application Process Applicants are encouraged to carefully prepare their applications. Strong applications typically demonstrate: Interest in public health service. Leadership potential. Technical laboratory expertise. Volunteer and community service experience. Strong personal statements. Commitment to public health careers. Important Dates Application Opens June 22, 2026 Application Deadline August 2, 2026 Interview Period October 26 – November 10, 2026 Fellowship Start Date July 2027 Late applications will not be considered. Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit all required materials well before the deadline. For more information about this opportunity click here Why You Should Apply The CDC Laboratory Leadership Service Fellowship is among the premier public health laboratory leadership programs in the United States. It offers a rare opportunity to combine advanced scientific expertise with leadership development, public health service, outbreak response experience, and professional growth. For scientists who aspire to influence public health policy, strengthen laboratory systems, and lead future responses to health emergencies, the LLS Fellowship provides an exceptional pathway toward a meaningful and impactful career. For additional details and applications, visit the CDC Laboratory Leadership Service website. Deadline: August 2, 2026. Discover global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post CDC Laboratory Leadership Service (LLS) Fellowship 2027 Applications Open: Fully Paid Two-Year Public Health Laboratory Leadership Program in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: USIU-Africa Financial Aid and Scholarships 2026: Comprehensive Funding Opportunities for Kenyan and International Students
summary: Pursuing higher education can be a significant financial investment, but the United States International University-Africa (USIU-Africa) is committed to ensuring that talented and deserving students have access to quality education regardless of their financial circumstances. Through an extensive range of scholarships, grants, bursaries, and financial aid programs, USIU-Africa continues to support students from Kenya, Africa, and around the world. Located in Nairobi, Kenya, USIU-Africa is one of the continent s leading private universities, offering internationally recognized programs and attracting students from over 70 countries. To make higher education more accessible, the institution provides several funding opportunities designed to reward academic excellence, leadership, community engagement, and financial need. Why Choose USIU-Africa? USIU-Africa is renowned for: Internationally accredited degree programs. Diverse student population representing more than 70 countries. Strong emphasis on leadership and innovation. World-class faculty and facilities. Extensive partnerships with universities and organizations worldwide. Commitment to promoting access to quality higher education. Recognizing that finances can be a barrier to education, the university has established numerous financial aid initiatives to support students throughout their academic journey. Types of Financial Aid Available at USIU-Africa USIU-Africa offers various forms of financial assistance, including: Scholarships Grants Bursaries Tuition assistance programs Donor-funded awards Need-based financial aid Merit-based scholarships These opportunities are intended to help students pursue their studies without excessive financial burdens. Merit-Based Scholarships Students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance may qualify for merit-based scholarships. These awards are designed to: Recognize academic excellence. Encourage high achievement. Reward outstanding performance. Support talented students pursuing undergraduate and graduate studies. Recipients are often required to maintain strong academic standing throughout their studies. Need-Based Financial Aid USIU-Africa understands that many capable students face economic challenges. Through need-based financial assistance, the university seeks to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent deserving students from obtaining a quality education. Need-based aid considers: Family income levels. Financial circumstances. Educational expenses. Ability to pay tuition fees. Students applying for this form of assistance may be required to provide supporting financial documents. Donor-Supported Scholarships Several organizations and partners collaborate with USIU-Africa to provide scholarship opportunities for deserving students. These programs may support: Women in leadership. Refugees and displaced students. Students from marginalized communities. Students pursuing particular academic disciplines. Community development initiatives. Each donor-funded scholarship has its own eligibility criteria and application procedures. Scholarships for Refugees and Vulnerable Students USIU-Africa has become widely recognized for supporting refugees and displaced populations through educational opportunities. These initiatives aim to: Promote inclusion. Increase access to higher education. Empower disadvantaged communities. Develop future leaders capable of transforming society. Students benefiting from such programs often receive comprehensive support that extends beyond tuition assistance. Leadership and Community Impact Scholarships The university values leadership and social responsibility. Some scholarships are awarded to students who have demonstrated: Leadership potential. Volunteerism. Community service. Social impact initiatives. Entrepreneurial activities. Such programs seek to nurture future leaders who will contribute positively to society. Sports Scholarships Talented athletes may also qualify for sports-related scholarships. These scholarships help students: Balance academics with athletics. Represent the university in competitions. Continue developing their sporting talents. Sports scholarships recognize excellence in various disciplines and encourage holistic development. International Student Support USIU-Africa attracts students from across Africa and beyond. International students can access several forms of financial aid depending on: Academic merit. Country of origin. Financial need. Sponsorship agreements. Donor requirements. This commitment strengthens the university s multicultural learning environment. Benefits of USIU-Africa Financial Aid Students who receive financial assistance enjoy numerous advantages, including: Reduced Financial Burden Scholarships and grants help offset tuition costs and other educational expenses. Access to Quality Education Recipients gain access to internationally accredited programs and world-class facilities. Leadership Development Many programs encourage leadership and community engagement. Career Advancement Opportunities Students graduate with qualifications recognized globally, increasing employability and career prospects. Diverse Learning Environment Scholars become part of a multicultural academic community with students from more than 70 countries. Application Process Students interested in financial aid should: Apply for admission to USIU-Africa. Review available scholarships and funding opportunities. Confirm eligibility requirements. Prepare supporting documents. Complete the appropriate financial aid application forms. Submit all required materials within specified deadlines. Because deadlines vary by scholarship, applicants are advised to regularly monitor the official USIU-Africa Financial Aid webpage for updates and announcements. Why USIU-Africa Scholarships Matter Access to education transforms lives and communities. Through its robust financial aid programs, USIU-Africa demonstrates its commitment to inclusivity, academic excellence, and social impact. By investing in talented students regardless of economic background, the university is helping shape future leaders, innovators, researchers, and professionals who will contribute to Africa s development and the global community. Prospective students are encouraged to explore the various scholarships and funding opportunities available and begin their journey toward earning a world-class education at one of Africa s premier universities. For complete details and current opportunities, visit the official USIU-Africa Financial Aid page . title: Swiss Researcher–Sciencepreneur Collaboration Grant (RSCG) 2026 Opens Applications: Up to CHF 20,000 to Support Innovation Partnerships Between Switzerland and Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Researchers and innovation-driven entrepreneurs across Switzerland and Sub-Saharan Africa now have an exciting opportunity to collaborate through the Researcher–Sciencepreneur Collaboration Grant (RSCG) 2026. Launched by the Swiss Leading House Africa, the initiative seeks to bridge academic research and entrepreneurship by supporting partnerships that transform scientific knowledge into impactful innovations. The RSCG 2026 program is designed to create stronger links between researchers and science-based ventures, helping innovative ideas move from laboratories into real-world applications capable of addressing societal and economic challenges. What is the Researcher–Sciencepreneur Collaboration Grant (RSCG) 2026? The Researcher–Sciencepreneur Collaboration Grant is a funding initiative established by the Swiss Leading House Africa. The program aims to promote collaboration between: Researchers and academics. Science-based startups and entrepreneurs. Innovation ecosystems in Switzerland and Sub-Saharan Africa. Institutions interested in translating research into practical solutions. The grant encourages teams to jointly develop projects with clear pathways toward commercialization, societal impact, and sustainable development. Funding Available Selected projects can receive grants of up to CHF 20,000 to support collaborative activities and innovation development. The funding is intended to strengthen partnerships and accelerate the transformation of scientific ideas into market-ready or socially impactful solutions. Objectives of the Program The initiative seeks to: Foster stronger research and entrepreneurial collaborations. Support science-based innovation ecosystems. Create sustainable partnerships between Switzerland and Africa. Enable ventures to establish a credible pathway to impact. Promote knowledge exchange and capacity building. Encourage research commercialization and innovation-driven entrepreneurship. Who Can Participate? The grant supports collaborative teams involving: Researchers from Switzerland. Researchers from Sub-Saharan Africa. Sciencepreneurs and startup founders. Innovation ventures based on scientific discoveries. Academic institutions. Technology-focused enterprises. A project must include: At least one Swiss partner. At least one Sub-Saharan African partner. Eligible African Countries The program welcomes applicants from numerous countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, including: Malawi South Africa Kenya Uganda Rwanda Tanzania Zambia Zimbabwe Nigeria Ghana Ethiopia Mozambique Botswana Senegal Cameroon Namibia Sierra Leone Liberia Madagascar And many other eligible countries within the region. Application Requirements Applications are submitted by the Swiss lead applicant through the F6S platform. African partners participate as co-applicants. Applications should include: 1. Venture Description Applicants must explain the startup or innovation venture involved. 2. Project Outline The proposal should clearly define: Objectives. Activities. Expected outcomes. Impact pathway. 3. Pitch Deck Teams must provide a concise presentation highlighting: The problem being addressed. The innovation. Market opportunities. Collaboration strategy. 4. Two-Minute Video Applicants are encouraged to introduce their collaboration through a short video featuring both partners whenever possible. Why Apply? Successful participants will benefit from: Financial Support Receive up to CHF 20,000 to advance collaborative innovation activities. International Partnerships Build long-term relationships between African and Swiss institutions and ventures. Knowledge Exchange Share expertise, technologies, and research capabilities. Innovation Development Transform research findings into practical solutions with commercial or societal impact. Increased Visibility Gain recognition through a prestigious international program supported by Swiss institutions. Future Funding Opportunities Strong collaborations established through RSCG may pave the way for larger research and innovation grants. Application Timeline Call Opening: May 2026 Application Deadline: 31 August 2026 (Midnight CET) Eligibility Screening: Week of 7 September 2026 How to Apply Applications are submitted through the F6S platform by the Swiss lead applicant. Applicants should begin preparations early to allow sufficient time for developing a strong proposal, pitch deck, and collaboration video. Find more information about this opportunity on research+ official website Deadline: 31 August 2026 Final Thoughts The Researcher–Sciencepreneur Collaboration Grant (RSCG) 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for innovators, researchers, and science-based entrepreneurs across Switzerland and Sub-Saharan Africa. By supporting cross-border collaboration and innovation, the program seeks to turn scientific discoveries into practical solutions that create lasting social and economic impact. title: Global Citizen Small Business Program: Funding and Support Opportunities for Entrepreneurs Looking to Scale Their Businesses
summary: Entrepreneurs and small business owners around the world are constantly searching for opportunities that provide not only funding but also access to networks, visibility, and support systems that can help them scale their impact. The Global Citizen Small Business Program is one such initiative aimed at empowering businesses and entrepreneurs who are creating solutions to pressing challenges while driving economic growth and social impact. As small businesses continue to play a vital role in creating jobs and strengthening economies, programs like this are designed to provide entrepreneurs with resources and opportunities needed to accelerate growth and expand their reach. What is the Global Citizen Small Business Program? The Global Citizen Small Business Program is an initiative by Global Citizen that supports entrepreneurs and small businesses by providing opportunities that help them grow, increase their impact, and contribute to stronger and more inclusive economies. Small businesses are the backbone of many communities, and supporting entrepreneurs is essential for promoting innovation, creating employment, and driving sustainable development. Through this initiative, Global Citizen seeks to connect businesses with opportunities that enable them to thrive and scale. Why Small Businesses Matter Small businesses contribute significantly to economic development. They: Create employment opportunities. Drive innovation and entrepreneurship. Support local communities and economies. Promote inclusive growth. Help address social and environmental challenges. Strengthen resilience and economic sustainability. By investing in entrepreneurs and emerging businesses, the Global Citizen Small Business Program aims to unlock greater impact and foster long-term growth. Benefits of the Global Citizen Small Business Program Participants may gain access to several valuable opportunities, including: 1. Funding Opportunities Businesses may access financial support that can help them: Expand operations. Invest in new technologies. Increase production capacity. Improve service delivery. Reach new markets. 2. Business Growth Support Entrepreneurs can benefit from: Business development support. Growth strategies. Capacity-building opportunities. Market expansion guidance. Tools and resources for scaling operations. 3. Networking Opportunities Participants may connect with: Industry leaders. Investors. Fellow entrepreneurs. Business experts. Strategic partners. These relationships can open doors to collaborations and long-term growth opportunities. 4. Increased Visibility Global Citizen s international platform can provide exposure to innovative businesses and impactful entrepreneurs, helping them gain recognition and attract additional opportunities. Why Entrepreneurs Should Consider Applying Competition for business funding is often intense, making programs like the Global Citizen Small Business Program especially valuable. Entrepreneurs with scalable businesses and a clear vision for growth can benefit significantly from access to funding and support systems. Strong applications typically demonstrate: A clear business model. Market demand for products or services. Growth potential. Social or economic impact. Innovation and sustainability. Ability to scale. How to Apply Interested entrepreneurs should visit the official Global Citizen Small Business Program website to review: Eligibility requirements. Program details. Application procedures. Important dates and announcements. Entrepreneurs can Apply here Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and ensure they provide complete and compelling applications that clearly communicate their business model, impact, and growth plans. Final Thoughts Small businesses are powerful engines of innovation and economic transformation. Through the Global Citizen Small Business Program, entrepreneurs have an opportunity to access resources that can help them grow sustainable businesses, create jobs, and contribute to stronger communities. For entrepreneurs seeking funding, visibility, and growth support, this initiative represents an important opportunity to accelerate their journey and maximize their impact. title: UNESCO Youth for Peace Intercultural Leadership Programme 2026: Apply for Funding, Mentorship and Global Leadership Opportunities
summary: Key Details Host Organization: UNESCO Programme: Youth for Peace: Intercultural Leadership Programme 2026 Theme: Human Connection and Dialogue in the Age of Artificial Intelligence Eligibility: Emerging leaders aged 25–45 years from around the world Funding: USD 10,000 seed funding for selected participants community initiatives Number of Participants: 50 Young Leaders Programme Components: Leadership training, mentorship, community action, global engagement, and alumni network Application Deadline: 19 July 2026 (23:59 CET, Paris Time) Notification of Results: Mid-October 2026 Working Language: English Apply: Official UNESCO Application Portal Lead Change Through Intercultural Leadership As societies become increasingly interconnected yet fragmented by polarisation, inequality, and rapid technological change, there is a growing need for leaders who can bridge divides, foster trust, and mobilise collective action across differences. The Youth for Peace: UNESCO Intercultural Leadership Programme is UNESCO’s flagship initiative for emerging leaders who are driving social cohesion, inclusion, and peace through intercultural dialogue. The programme is founded on the conviction that intercultural dialogue is not simply a communication tool — it is a transformative form of leadership that equips people and communities to navigate complexity, build trust across differences, and develop shared solutions to common challenges. Through leadership development, mentorship, community action, and global engagement, the programme builds a new generation of intercultural leaders who translate dialogue into meaningful change in their communities and beyond. Shutterstock 2026 Theme: Human Connection and Dialogue in the Age of Artificial Intelligence Artificial Intelligence is transforming how people communicate, learn, work, and engage across cultures — creating new opportunities for participation, education, and connection while raising urgent questions about trust, inclusion, representation, and equality. Participants will explore how AI is reshaping intercultural relationships and social cohesion, engaging with challenges including: M isinformation, hate speech, and online polarisation A lgorithmic bias, stereotyping, and digital discrimination U nequal access to technology, infrastructure, education, and digital skills T he representation of diverse cultures, languages, and knowledge systems within AI At the same time, participants will examine how AI can advance intercultural understanding through multilingual communication, accessibility, civic participation, cultural preservation, conflict prevention, and new forms of cross-border collaboration. Through this lens, participants will develop dialogue-based responses that place human connection and intercultural understanding at the centre of technological change. Selected participants will join a global community of intercultural leaders and receive the support, resources, and platform to advance dialogue-driven initiatives, contribute to international policy conversations, and help build more inclusive and peaceful societies. The programme forms part of UNESCO’s Road to Peace: Dialogue and Action for Tolerance and Intercultural Understanding initiative. The Leadership Journey Three interconnected phases combining leadership development, practical action, and global engagement. PHASE 1 Strengthen your leadership practice An immersive learning journey designed to deepen participants’ capacity to lead across differences and mobilise dialogue as a catalyst for change. Preparatory Training Programme A structured series of interactive online workshops building the competencies intercultural leaders need to drive change in complex, diverse, and rapidly evolving environments. Topics may include: Intercultural leadership in practice — leading across differences, building trust in diverse settings, and mobilising collective action through dialogue Strategic communication and influence — framing narratives for impact, communicating across cultural and linguistic boundaries, and engaging stakeholders at multiple levels Intercultural mediation and conflict transformation — facilitating dialogue in contested spaces, navigating disagreement constructively, and supporting reconciliation through intercultural approaches Foresight, anticipation, and adaptive leadership — reading complex social and technological trends, anticipating change, and designing responses that are resilient and forward-looking Technology as a tool for dialogue — exploring how digital platforms and AI can expand the reach of intercultural dialogue, support multilingual communication, and open new spaces for participation and collaboration AI as a resource for intercultural leaders — using AI tools ethically and critically to design, communicate, and amplify leadership initiatives while centering inclusion and intercultural sensitivity Designing dialogue-based initiatives for impact — developing theory of change, engaging communities, and building initiatives that generate measurable social outcomes PHASE 2 Lead an intercultural action initiative Participants apply their learning by designing and leading an intercultural dialogue initiative that addresses the programme’s annual theme. Seed funding: USD 10,000 Upon approval of their action plan, participants receive seed funding to translate their leadership vision into practical community action. Initiatives must: Align with the theme Human Connection and Dialogue in the Age of Artificial Intelligence U se intercultural dialogue as a core methodology B e implemented within six months at local or national level Throughout implementation, participants continue to receive mentorship, learning opportunities, and peer support. Definition of intercultural dialogue “A process undertaken to realize transformative communication that requires space or opportunities for engagement and a diverse group of participants committed to values such as mutual respect, empathy, and a willingness to consider different perspectives.” — UNESCO Institute for Economics Peace, 2020 PHASE 3 Amplify impact and influence global conversations Beyond community-level action, participants contribute to broader global discussions on peace, inclusion, and intercultural leadership. UNESCO Global Youth Dialogue for Peace A convening of emerging leaders and decision-makers from around the world, providing opportunities to: P resent initiative outcomes and lessons learned E xchange innovative practices and approaches E ngage directly with policymakers and institutional leaders C ontribute youth perspectives to global discussions on social cohesion and peace Global alumni network Membership in the Youth for Peace Global Alumni Network, with ongoing opportunities for collaboration, advocacy, and leadership development. Knowledge and policy contributions Access to UNESCO tools, methodologies, and resources, with opportunities to contribute insights to the UNESCO Global Intercultural Action Observatory, informing future policy and advocacy efforts. Programme Timeline English is the official working language of the programme. All applications, proposals, and programme communications must be submitted in English. UNESCO Who Should Apply UNESCO is seeking emerging leaders who demonstrate a strong commitment to dialogue, inclusion, and positive social change. Applicants must: Be between 25 and 45 years old as of 22 October 2026 H ave a demonstrated record of leadership in their field S how commitment to intercultural dialogue and social impact D emonstrate openness to self-reflection, dialogue across differences, and collaborative learning H ave the potential to influence and create positive change in their communities B e available to participate fully in all programme components Inability to participate fully in the programme will result in non-selection. Selection Process Step 1 — Submit your application Complete the online application form with all required documentation by 19 July 2026, 23:59 CET (Paris time). The use of AI tools in preparing applications for this programme is permitted for support purposes (e.g. structuring ideas, refining language, or improving clarity). Applicants are expected to take ownership of all submitted content and ensure that their application authentically reflects their own perspectives, experiences, and motivations. Applications that fail to demonstrate sufficient personal engagement may be deemed ineligible. Click HERE to apply. Step 2 — Eligibility screening Applications are reviewed against programme eligibility requirements. Step 3 — Technical assessment Eligible applications are evaluated by an international jury against programme assessment criteria. Step 4 — Final selection The cohort of 50 Young Leaders will be notified by mid-October 2026. Apply now and become part of the next generation of intercultural leaders. If you are ready to strengthen your leadership, mobilise dialogue as a force for change, and contribute to a more inclusive and peaceful world, we encourage you to apply. Through the Youth for Peace programme, you will gain the skills, resources, networks, and platform to lead dialogue-driven action in your community and join a growing global movement working for peace and social cohesion. Click HERE to apply. Application Deadline Deadline: 19 July 2026 (23:59 CET, Paris Time) Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all programme requirements before submitting their applications. Please read the Terms and Conditions carefully before submitting. Looking for More Leadership Opportunities? Explore more fully funded fellowships, conferences, grants, internships, scholarships, and leadership programmes for young people worldwide on Opportunities For Youth . Source: UNESCO The post UNESCO Youth for Peace Intercultural Leadership Programme 2026: Apply for Funding, Mentorship and Global Leadership Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Young Women’s Advisory Council (YWAC) 2026: Apply to Join FORWARD’s Youth Leadership Body
summary: The Young Women’s Advisory Council (YWAC) is now accepting applications for its 2026 cohort. This prestigious leadership opportunity, hosted by FORWARD—the African women‑led organisation dedicated to ending violence against women and girls—invites passionate young women aged 18–30 from African, Black, and minoritised communities in the UK to step into meaningful leadership, advocacy, and community‑impact roles. FORWARD has long been recognised as a leading organisation championing dignity, equality, and justice for African women and girls. Through its programmes addressing female genital mutilation (FGM), child marriage, domestic abuse, and sexual violence, FORWARD continues to empower communities and amplify the voices of young women. The Young Women’s Advisory Council plays a central role in shaping this mission. What is the Young Women’s Advisory Council (YWAC)? The YWAC is a core leadership body within FORWARD’s organisational structure. It sits at the heart of the organisation’s Young Women’s Leadership Programme and Youth Hub activities. Members serve as ambassadors, advocates, and strategic contributors who help guide the direction of FORWARD’s youth‑focused initiatives. YWAC members bring diverse experiences, skills, and motivations. Together, they form a collective force committed to transforming the lives of girls and women across African, Black, and minoritised communities. What YWAC Represents YWAC members are recognised as: FORWARD ambassadors — representing the organisation’s values and mission. Youth leaders — shaping programmes and influencing decision‑making. Change‑makers — using lived experiences to advocate for equality and justice. Each member contributes unique insights that help strengthen FORWARD’s work and ensure that young women’s voices remain central to programme design and delivery. Key Responsibilities of YWAC Members YWAC members work closely with FORWARD’s Youth Programme staff and take on a variety of strategic and operational responsibilities, including: Providing strategic advice and guidance on youth‑focused initiatives. Leading advocacy and campaign work within the Youth Hub. Representing FORWARD at events across the UK. Participating in consultations and research on issues affecting young African, Black, and minoritised women. Roles Available for 2026 FORWARD is recruiting for several leadership and operational positions within the Council: Co‑Chairs (x2) Secretary Operations Officer Finance and Fundraising Officer Communications Officer Outreach Officer Each role contributes to the smooth functioning, visibility, and impact of the Youth Advisory Council. Who Should Apply? You are encouraged to apply if you are: Passionate about women’s rights, dignity, and equality . Confident, outspoken, and committed to advocating for your rights . A strong communicator and collaborative team player. Open to learning, growth, and leadership development. Able to volunteer at least one day per month for YWAC activities. Creative, proactive, and full of new ideas. What You Gain from Joining YWAC Becoming a YWAC member offers a wide range of personal and professional benefits: Opportunity to pursue your passion and make a tangible difference in the lives of women and girls. Opportunity to build leadership, public speaking, and project development skills . Access to training and personal development opportunities. Opportunity to co‑design programmes , facilitate sessions, and deliver youth‑focused events. Opportunity to connect with like‑minded activists and build lifelong sisterhood networks. How to Apply Applicants must submit: A CV A 300‑word supporting statement titled “YWAC Application 2025” Indicate clearly which role you are applying for Deadline: Thursday 31st July 2026 at 12:00 PM (BST) Please note: This is a voluntary role , but a stipend is available for related activities. For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more volunteer opportunities here The post Young Women’s Advisory Council (YWAC) 2026: Apply to Join FORWARD’s Youth Leadership Body appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Designing Cities Initiative 2026 Remote Jobs: Three International NGO Career Opportunities for Professionals Worldwide
summary: The Global Designing Cities Initiative (GDCI) has announced three exciting remote employment opportunities for professionals passionate about transforming streets, improving urban mobility, promoting sustainable transportation, and creating safer, healthier, and more equitable cities worldwide. These international opportunities provide professionals with the chance to work remotely while contributing to high-impact urban development projects supported by globally recognized initiatives such as the Bloomberg Initiative for Global Road Safety (BIGRS), the Bloomberg Initiative for Cycling Infrastructure, and the Streets for Kids Program. Whether you are an early-career urban designer, an experienced program leader, or a professional interested in capacity building and training, GDCI offers rewarding career opportunities that combine technical expertise, global collaboration, and meaningful social impact. Applications are now open for three different remote positions, with deadlines in June and July 2026. About the Global Designing Cities Initiative (GDCI) Founded in 2014, the Global Designing Cities Initiative (GDCI) works to transform streets into safe, healthy, accessible, and equitable public spaces. Its work is built around the internationally recognized Global Street Design Guide, which has helped cities across the world improve transportation systems, strengthen public spaces, enhance road safety, and build local capacity for sustainable urban development. GDCI brings together planners, urban designers, transportation specialists, engineers, researchers, and policymakers to create people-first streets that improve quality of life in cities globally. The organization works closely with governments, local authorities, development partners, and international organizations to deliver technical assistance, policy guidance, training, and implementation support. 1. Program Associate (Junior Level) Location Remote (Work from Home) Preferred countries: Kenya South Africa Start Date Q3 2026 About the Role This junior-level position supports GDCI s regional technical assistance work across African cities. The Program Associate will work closely with Program Managers and multidisciplinary teams to coordinate projects, prepare technical designs, assist with workshops, support city engagements, and contribute to research and publications. The role combines urban design, transportation planning, project coordination, research, and stakeholder engagement. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will: Produce technical street design drawings and sketches. Support city street design projects. Review local and national design regulations. Conduct policy and technical research. Develop reports, presentations, and publications. Assist with data collection and field observations. Coordinate workshops and training sessions. Help organize community engagement activities. Prepare technical documentation. Collaborate with government officials and local partners. Support project implementation and evaluation. Ideal Candidate Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interest in transportation planning and urban design. Experience using AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite, SketchUp, or similar software. Excellent communication skills. Strong organizational abilities. Ability to work across multiple countries and time zones. Passion for creating safer and more sustainable cities. Preferred Experience Typical backgrounds include: Four years of relevant experience; or Two years of experience with a bachelor s degree; or A master s degree with little or no professional experience. 2. Associate Director – Programs Design (Regional) Location Remote Open to professionals based in: Africa Asia Europe Start Date Q3 2026 About the Position This senior leadership role sits within GDCI s Executive Team and oversees the successful delivery of funded global programs while maintaining the organization s high standards in urban street design. The Associate Director will help coordinate projects across more than 30 cities worldwide while ensuring technical quality, strong stakeholder engagement, budget oversight, and organizational leadership. Major Responsibilities The successful candidate will: Oversee major international programs. Supervise multidisciplinary teams. Ensure design quality across projects. Manage relationships with global funders. Oversee reporting and program budgets. Support executive leadership. Represent GDCI internationally. Help secure future partnerships and funding. Strengthen internal systems and operational excellence. Lead professional development of staff. Desired Profile Successful applicants should possess: Extensive experience managing complex international projects. Strong urban design knowledge. Leadership and people management experience. Excellent written and verbal communication. Strategic thinking and organizational management skills. Experience working with governments and international partners. Typical experience includes: 14 years of professional experience; or 12 years with a bachelor s degree; or 10 years with a master s degree. 3. Capacity Building Associate Location Remote Open to applicants from any time zone worldwide. Start Date Q3–Q4 2026 About the Position The Capacity Building Associate will help coordinate GDCI s global learning and training programs designed for governments, practitioners, engineers, planners, community organizations, and local leaders. The role focuses on developing engaging learning experiences that translate technical urban design concepts into practical training materials for diverse international audiences. Main Responsibilities The successful candidate will: Coordinate capacity-building resources. Support curriculum development. Develop workshop materials. Organize webinars and training sessions. Assist in producing online educational content. Coordinate multilingual learning materials. Support communications and digital learning resources. Research innovative instructional methods. Help improve global knowledge-sharing initiatives. Ideal Candidate Applicants should have: Passion for sustainable urban development. Excellent communication skills. Strong organizational abilities. Experience coordinating educational or training programs. Ability to work across cultures and international teams. Interest in instructional design and knowledge sharing. Benefits of Working with GDCI Employees joining GDCI may receive: Competitive salary based on country of hire. Comprehensive health coverage. Retirement benefits. Paid annual leave. Paid sick leave. Paid holidays. Flexible remote work. International collaboration opportunities. Professional growth and leadership development. Meaningful work that contributes to safer, healthier cities worldwide. Working Environment GDCI operates as a fully remote organization with staff working across multiple continents. Team members regularly collaborate across time zones while supporting projects in cities worldwide. Depending on the position, occasional international travel may be required to support technical assistance, workshops, conferences, and stakeholder engagement. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion GDCI is committed to maintaining an inclusive workplace where diversity is valued and respected. As a fiscally sponsored project of Rockefeller Philanthropy Advisors (RPA), the organization provides equal employment opportunities without discrimination based on race, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, veteran status, age, national origin, or any other protected characteristic. Application Requirements Depending on the position, applicants may be required to submit: Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume Cover letter or letter of interest Portfolio (for applicable positions) Additional samples demonstrating communication or technical skills Applications should be submitted through the official GDCI application portal. Application Deadlines Capacity Building Associate: 30 June 2026 Program Associate (Junior Level): 15 July 2026 Associate Director – Programs Design: 15 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Apply Here Why You Should Apply These opportunities provide professionals with the chance to contribute to transformative urban development initiatives while working remotely with one of the world s leading organizations in sustainable street design. Whether you are beginning your international career or looking to advance into executive leadership, GDCI offers a unique platform to collaborate with governments, international organizations, researchers, and city leaders in creating safer, more inclusive, and more sustainable cities around the world. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Global Designing Cities Initiative 2026 Remote Jobs: Three International NGO Career Opportunities for Professionals Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ford Foundation Technology Fellows Program 2026: $170,000 Salary Opportunities in AI, Philanthropy, Digital Safety and Security in New York, USA
summary: The Ford Foundation has officially opened applications for two highly prestigious Technology Fellow positions under its renowned Technology Fellows Program. These two-year fellowship opportunities are designed for experienced technology professionals who are passionate about social justice, public-interest technology, digital transformation, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, philanthropy, and societal impact. The Foundation is currently recruiting for: Technology Fellow: AI and Philanthropy Technology Fellow: Digital Safety and Security Both positions are based in New York City and offer competitive salaries ranging from $166,000 to $170,000 annually, comprehensive benefits, professional development opportunities, and the chance to influence one of the world s most respected philanthropic organizations. Applications for both roles must be submitted no later than July 6, 2026 . About the Ford Foundation For more than eight decades, the Ford Foundation has been one of the world s leading philanthropic organizations dedicated to advancing social justice, reducing inequality, strengthening democratic values, promoting international cooperation, and fostering human achievement. Headquartered in New York City, the Foundation operates globally through regional offices across: Africa Asia Latin America The Middle East The Foundation supports visionary leaders, organizations, and movements that are addressing some of the most pressing challenges facing humanity. Recognizing the growing influence of technology on society, the Ford Foundation has increasingly invested in building expertise at the intersection of technology, democracy, equity, social justice, and public policy. About the Ford Technology Fellows Program The Technology Fellows Program was established to strengthen sociotechnical capacity throughout the Foundation. A sociotechnical approach recognizes that technology and society continuously influence one another and that technological innovation must be evaluated alongside its social, ethical, political, and economic impacts. The program aims to: 1. Enrich Internal Perspectives Technology Fellows bring diverse technical expertise into the Foundation and help staff understand emerging technological opportunities and risks through a social justice lens. 2. Increase Technical Capacity The fellows strengthen organizational understanding of: Artificial Intelligence Digital Security Data Governance Emerging Technologies Technology Policy Responsible Innovation 3. Generate New Ideas and Networks The program encourages innovation by helping the Foundation build relationships with: Researchers Practitioners Technology experts Civil society organizations Social justice advocates Government institutions Philanthropic partners Technology Fellows act as both strategic advisors and technical experts, helping the Foundation navigate an increasingly complex technological landscape. Technology Fellow: AI and Philanthropy Overview The AI and Philanthropy Fellow will focus on helping the Foundation better understand and responsibly adopt artificial intelligence technologies. This role is primarily internal-facing and supports Foundation staff through strategic guidance, training, research, and organizational learning initiatives. Reporting directly to the Director of Technology and Society, the fellow will help create frameworks and practices that enable ethical, mission-aligned AI adoption throughout the organization. Key Responsibilities The selected fellow will: Develop AI Evaluation Frameworks Support Foundation teams in assessing AI tools. Build internal frameworks for responsible AI adoption. Evaluate ethical, operational, and sociotechnical implications of AI systems. Lead AI Training and Learning Design workshops and training programs. Build staff familiarity with AI technologies. Help employees critically evaluate AI effectiveness and risks. Promote responsible AI experimentation. Conduct Research and Analysis The fellow will monitor: Global AI developments Emerging AI technologies Policy and regulatory trends AI innovation in civil society AI applications in philanthropy Build Strategic Partnerships Responsibilities include: Engaging AI researchers Connecting with practitioners Collaborating with nonprofits Identifying innovative grantees Supporting mission-driven AI initiatives Produce Knowledge Resources The fellow will create: AI guidance documents Internal learning materials Strategic briefings Research reports Best-practice resources Desired Expertise Applicants should possess experience in: Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning Generative AI Responsible AI AI Governance Technology Strategy Organizational Change Philanthropic Innovation Technology Fellow: Digital Safety and Security Overview The Digital Safety and Security Fellow will focus on strengthening digital security capacity among the Foundation and its global network of grantee organizations. As civil society organizations face increasingly sophisticated cyber threats, surveillance risks, and information security challenges, this fellow will help expand the Foundation s ability to support secure and resilient organizations worldwide. Reporting to the Director of the Office of the Vice President for U.S. Programs, the fellow will work directly with program officers and grantee partners across multiple regions. Key Responsibilities Strengthen Digital Security Capacity The fellow will: Assess digital security needs. Recommend security improvements. Support technical assistance programs. Help organizations improve resilience against cyber threats. Build Ecosystem Capacity Responsibilities include: Identifying strategic gaps. Mapping digital security providers. Supporting field-building efforts. Developing networks across the public-interest technology sector. Support High-Risk Grantees The fellow will: Provide strategic guidance. Support digital risk mitigation. Enhance security preparedness. Improve organizational safety practices. Evaluate Emerging Technologies The role involves analyzing: Surveillance technologies Security software Privacy-enhancing technologies Emerging digital risks Cybersecurity solutions Desired Expertise Strong candidates may possess backgrounds in: Cybersecurity Information Security Digital Rights Privacy Protection Threat Intelligence Risk Management Surveillance Technologies Public Interest Technology Qualifications for Both Fellowship Positions Applicants for either position should demonstrate: Education Bachelor s degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience. Professional Experience Minimum of seven years of relevant professional experience. Technical Expertise Candidates should possess expertise in one or more of the following: Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning Generative AI Cybersecurity Digital Safety Technology Policy Information Security Public Interest Technology Additional Competencies Strong candidates should also demonstrate: Strategic thinking Excellent communication skills Research expertise Stakeholder engagement abilities Strong writing and presentation skills Cross-sector collaboration experience Ability to manage confidential information Leadership and teamwork skills Preferred Qualifications Although not required, the Foundation values applicants with: Change management experience Organizational transformation expertise International experience Familiarity with regions where Ford operates Understanding of geopolitical issues Salary and Compensation The Ford Foundation offers exceptional compensation. Annual Salary Range Minimum: $166,000 Maximum: $170,000 Final compensation depends on: Experience Qualifications Internal equity considerations Benefits Package Employees receive a comprehensive package that includes: Health and Wellness Benefits Medical insurance Dental insurance Vision coverage Wellness support programs Time-Off Benefits Vacation leave Personal leave Sick leave Extended holiday leave Wellness days Family Benefits Generous parental leave Adoption support Foster parent support Surrogacy support Childcare assistance resources Elder care support resources Retirement Benefits The Foundation offers a highly competitive retirement package featuring employee and employer contributions of up to 13%. Professional Development Employees also benefit from: Career development opportunities Leadership growth Skills enhancement programs Networking opportunities Access to global experts Work Location and Working Model Both positions are based in: New York City, USA The Foundation operates a hybrid working model requiring employees to work in the office three days per week. For the Digital Safety and Security Fellow, domestic and international travel of approximately 10–20% may be required. Work Authorization Requirements Applicants should note that: Visa sponsorship is not available. Visa sponsorship will not be available in the future. Candidates must already possess legal authorization to work in the United States. Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion The Ford Foundation strongly believes that diversity strengthens organizations and communities. Employment decisions are made without regard to: Race Religion Gender National origin Disability Sexual orientation Age Veteran status Marital status The Foundation also welcomes applications from formerly incarcerated individuals and provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities. Why You Should Apply Few opportunities allow technology professionals to combine advanced technical expertise with global social impact at this scale. These Technology Fellow positions offer: Exceptional salaries of up to $170,000 annually. Direct influence on global philanthropy. Opportunities to shape responsible technology adoption. Exposure to leading social justice initiatives. Access to influential global networks. Meaningful work addressing societal challenges. Whether your expertise lies in artificial intelligence or digital security, these fellowships offer a rare opportunity to help shape the future of technology and social change. Application Deadline July 6, 2026 Interested applicants must submit both: A Resume A Cover Letter Applications submitted without both documents may not be considered. Learn More More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Ford Foundation Technology Fellows Program 2026: $170,000 Salary Opportunities in AI, Philanthropy, Digital Safety and Security in New York, USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CIP Fellowship Programme 2026: $14,000 Funding Opportunity for Emerging Researchers with Disabilities from LMICs
summary: Emerging researchers with disabilities from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) now have an outstanding opportunity to strengthen their research skills, expand their policy influence, and contribute to disability-inclusive development through the CIP Fellowship Programme 2026. Applications are currently open for this highly impactful 12-month online fellowship, which aims to empower persons with disabilities who are passionate about research, social policy, disability rights, and inclusive development. Through mentorship, financial support, specialized training, and access to a global network, fellows will gain the tools necessary to become influential voices in disability policy and evidence-based advocacy. The fellowship begins on October 1, 2026 , and applications must be submitted by July 31, 2026, at midnight EDT . What Is the CIP Fellowship Programme? The CIP Fellowship Programme is a year-long online initiative designed to strengthen the research and policy skills of emerging researchers with disabilities living in low- and middle-income countries. The programme focuses on building local research capacity while ensuring that persons with disabilities are actively involved in generating evidence, influencing policies, and shaping disability-inclusive development initiatives. Unlike fellowships designed for senior academics, this programme specifically targets individuals who already possess basic research experience and want to further develop their expertise, professional networks, and leadership potential. The fellowship recognizes that persons with disabilities are uniquely positioned to conduct research that reflects lived experiences and local realities. By supporting emerging researchers, the programme contributes to more inclusive and representative disability research worldwide. Fellowship Objectives The CIP Fellowship Programme is built around three key objectives. 1. Strengthening Local Research Capacity The programme seeks to empower researchers with disabilities to: Design locally relevant research projects Generate evidence rooted in lived experiences Address disability-related challenges within their own countries Produce research that informs policy and practice By supporting local researchers, the fellowship ensures that disability policy discussions are informed by context-specific evidence rather than external assumptions. 2. Connecting Fellows to Global Policy Discussions Selected fellows will become part of international conversations on: Disability rights Inclusive development Social protection Community support systems Independent living Inclusive policymaking The fellowship aligns with the principles of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) and helps ensure that fellows can contribute to global disability policy discussions. 3. Mentoring Disability Advocates and Researchers The programme provides structured mentorship to individuals working in disability advocacy, policy development, and social research. Through expert guidance, fellows will: Strengthen research skills Expand professional networks Improve policy engagement capabilities Enhance their ability to influence disability-inclusive policies Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support. Monthly Fellowship Stipend Each fellow will receive: USD $1,000 per month Funding provided for 12 months This represents a total stipend of: USD $12,000 over the fellowship period Additional Research and Professional Development Funding Fellows may also access: Up to USD $2,000 for research-related costs Conference participation support Funding for additional training opportunities Sponsorship for professional development activities Reasonable Accommodation Support The programme is committed to accessibility and inclusion. Reasonable accommodation costs necessary for participation will be fully covered, helping ensure equal access for all fellows. Expert Training Curriculum Participants gain access to: A structured online training programme Sessions led by leading international researchers Capacity-building workshops Research methodology training Policy engagement guidance Individual Mentorship Each fellow is matched with a mentor who will: Guide their research project Provide professional advice Support skill development Offer feedback throughout the fellowship year Global Networking Opportunities Fellows also receive opportunities to: Participate in CIP research projects Collaborate with international researchers Join a growing network of disability advocates Engage with policy professionals worldwide Research Areas of Interest The fellowship is seeking applicants interested in disability-related research and policy issues. Priority areas include: Disability Data Research on: Disability statistics Data collection methods Disability measurement Evidence-based policymaking Social Protection Projects focused on: Income support Disability benefits Social welfare systems Poverty reduction strategies Care and Support Research examining: Care systems Family support Personal assistance services Long-term support arrangements Inclusive Budgeting Studies exploring: Public expenditure Disability-inclusive budgeting Resource allocation Fiscal inclusion Community Support Systems Research on: Local support structures Community-based services Inclusive development approaches Social participation Independent Living Projects focused on: Autonomy Accessibility Community inclusion Rights-based support systems New Disability Data Fellowship Track In addition to the standard fellowship programme, CIP is recruiting participants for a specialized Disability Data Fellowship. This innovative track focuses on strengthening skills related to: Quantitative data collection Disability-inclusive data systems Data interpretation Data-driven advocacy Statistical analysis Research communication Fellows will receive practical experience and training on how disability data can be used to promote inclusion and influence policy decisions. Participants will also develop the capacity to support governments, organizations, and institutions in collecting and utilizing disability-inclusive data. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is looking for individuals who meet the following criteria: Required Eligibility Applicants must: Be a person with a disability Be based in a low- and middle-income country or the Global South Hold an undergraduate degree Possess basic research skills Be interested in social policy and disability equality Be connected to the disability movement Be motivated to strengthen research skills Have sufficient English proficiency for participation Language Requirements Applicants should have: Working written English skills Ability to understand everyday English communication Basic spoken English communication skills Importantly, spoken English proficiency is not required for sign language users, as interpretation support will be provided. No Age Restrictions One of the most inclusive aspects of the programme is that there is: No minimum age beyond educational requirements No upper age limit Applicants of all ages are encouraged to apply. Who Is Not Eligible? The fellowship is not intended for: Senior researchers Established academics Researchers with extensive United Nations agency experience Applicants whose work is unrelated to disability issues Applicants from countries restricted under OFAC regulations Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following materials. 1. Research Proposal (Maximum Three Pages) The proposal must focus on disability policy in the applicant s own country and include: Introduction Research question Methodology Data and methods Expected results Timeline demonstrating completion within 12 months Formatting requirements: Arial font size 12 2.5 cm margins on all sides Maximum three pages 2. Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Applicants should provide a current CV outlining: Educational background Research experience Professional achievements Relevant disability advocacy work 3. Cover Letter The cover letter should explain: Motivation for applying Research interests Career goals Commitment to disability inclusion 4. Two References Applicants must provide two professional or academic references who can speak to their qualifications and potential. Guidance on AI Use The programme does not prohibit the use of artificial intelligence tools. However, applicants are strongly advised not to rely on AI-generated: Research questions Research methodologies Instead, CIP encourages applicants to present their original ideas clearly and concisely, preferably using bullet points where appropriate. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Applicants should ensure that all required documents are included before submission. Important Deadline Application Deadline July 31, 2026 (Midnight EDT) Late applications may not be considered, so interested candidates are encouraged to prepare and submit their materials well before the deadline. Final Thoughts The CIP Fellowship Programme 2026 represents a transformative opportunity for emerging researchers with disabilities across low- and middle-income countries. With financial support worth up to USD $14,000, expert mentorship, accessibility accommodations, specialized training, and access to a global network of disability advocates and researchers, the programme equips fellows with the skills and resources needed to influence policy and advance disability rights. For aspiring researchers committed to creating inclusive societies and evidence-based disability policies, this fellowship offers a unique platform to develop expertise, strengthen leadership capacity, and contribute to meaningful change at local, national, and international levels. For more global fellowship opportunities click here The post CIP Fellowship Programme 2026: $14,000 Funding Opportunity for Emerging Researchers with Disabilities from LMICs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Pallas Foundation Fellowship Fall 2026: Paid National Security Fellowship for Future Defense, AI, Cyber, and Technology Leaders
summary: Are you interested in national security, defense innovation, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, emerging technologies, public policy, or government affairs? The Fall 2026 Pallas Foundation Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for students, recent graduates, and early-career professionals to gain direct exposure to some of the most influential leaders, organizations, and technologies shaping the future of national security. Applications are now open for the highly competitive 23rd Cohort of the Pallas Foundation Fellowship , a prestigious program designed to provide participants with hands-on experience working at the intersection of national security, technology, consulting, and policymaking. The fellowship provides a unique gateway into the national security ecosystem, allowing fellows to engage with senior government officials, leading technology companies, venture capital firms, and policy experts while developing valuable professional skills and industry knowledge. What Is the Pallas Foundation Fellowship? The Pallas Foundation Fellowship is a professional development and work-based learning program that allows fellows to work alongside the team at Pallas Advisors, a strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of national security, defense, technology, and government. Fellows become active contributors to the organization s operations and client-facing work while gaining firsthand exposure to the defense and national security sectors. The program is designed for ambitious individuals who want to understand how government, technology companies, investors, and policymakers collaborate to address evolving security challenges in an increasingly complex global environment. Why This Fellowship Matters The national security landscape is rapidly evolving due to advancements in: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Cybersecurity Space technologies Defense innovation Advanced computing Emerging technologies Critical infrastructure protection Intelligence and information operations Organizations across government and industry need talented professionals who understand both policy and technology. The Pallas Foundation Fellowship offers participants the opportunity to build that expertise while learning directly from leaders operating at the forefront of these fields. Fellowship Responsibilities As a Pallas Fellow, participants will play an active role in supporting the firm s daily operations and strategic initiatives. Key responsibilities include: 1. Conducting Research Fellows will: Perform tailored research on national security issues Analyze emerging technology trends Support policy-related projects Gather intelligence and background information for clients and stakeholders 2. Developing Client Products Participants will assist in: Preparing briefing materials Drafting information papers Creating presentations Producing analytical reports Supporting strategic communications initiatives 3. Supporting Business Development Fellows will contribute to: Client engagement activities Market research efforts Strategic growth initiatives Opportunity identification and analysis 4. Administrative and Operational Support Like all members of the team, fellows will help with: Internal coordination Project management support Administrative tasks Organizational initiatives Exclusive Exposure to National Security Leaders One of the most valuable aspects of the fellowship is the opportunity to interact with senior leaders from across the national security ecosystem. Fellows may engage with professionals and decision-makers from: Department of Defense (DoD) National Security Council (NSC) Intelligence Community Federal agencies Defense organizations Technology companies Venture capital firms Nonprofit institutions Policy organizations This level of exposure provides participants with insights that are difficult to obtain through traditional academic programs or entry-level positions. Work With Cutting-Edge Technology Companies The fellowship places participants at the intersection of technology and national security. Fellows gain valuable exposure to organizations working in areas such as: Artificial Intelligence Cybersecurity Defense technology Space technology Advanced software systems Emerging technologies Innovation ecosystems Venture-backed startups This experience helps participants understand how technological innovation is transforming defense, intelligence, and security operations. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive a range of professional and financial benefits. Competitive Compensation Participants receive: Competitive hourly pay Paid professional experience Valuable industry exposure Professional Development The fellowship includes: Weekly professional development programming Career mentoring opportunities Skills development workshops Industry insights and networking events Career Advancement Opportunities Participants can strengthen their: Research capabilities Analytical skills Writing and communication abilities Strategic thinking skills Understanding of national security policy Professional networks Flexible Work Structure The program offers: Part-time schedule Approximately 20–30 hours per week In-person opportunities Virtual participation options Fellowship Location The fellowship is available in: Washington, D.C. Pallas Advisors operates from its Washington, D.C. office and employs more than 30 professionals working across national security, technology, consulting, and policy sectors. Virtual Participation For qualified candidates who may not be located in Washington, D.C., virtual participation opportunities are also available. Desired Qualifications The ideal candidate is: Energetic and motivated Highly detail-oriented Interested in national security and defense issues Comfortable conducting research and analysis Skilled in written communication Capable of preparing presentations and briefing materials While not required, experience or knowledge in the following areas may strengthen an application: Defense industry Technology startups Venture capital Early-stage investing Security policy Technology policy Artificial intelligence Cybersecurity International affairs Why Students and Early-Career Professionals Should Apply The Pallas Foundation Fellowship is more than a work experience—it is a launchpad into one of the most influential professional ecosystems in the United States. Participants gain: Direct exposure to senior government leaders. Experience working on real-world national security challenges. Access to technology and defense innovation networks. Professional development opportunities. Valuable consulting and analytical experience. Connections with leaders in government, industry, and investment communities. A competitive advantage for future careers in defense, intelligence, policy, consulting, and technology. Potential Career Paths After the Fellowship The experience gained through the fellowship can support careers in: National Security Intelligence Analysis Defense Policy Government Affairs Public Policy Cybersecurity Artificial Intelligence Strategy Technology Consulting Venture Capital Defense Technology Startups International Affairs Strategic Advisory Services Application Deadline Applicants should submit their applications before the official closing date. Important Date Application Deadline: Friday, July 10, 2026 Closing Time: 12:00 PM EST Late applications may not be considered. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Final Thoughts The Fall 2026 Pallas Foundation Fellowship represents a rare opportunity for students, recent graduates, and emerging professionals to gain meaningful experience at the intersection of national security, defense innovation, public policy, and emerging technologies. With paid work experience, access to senior government and industry leaders, professional development programming, and exposure to some of the most important challenges facing national security today, the fellowship provides an outstanding pathway into one of the most dynamic and impactful career fields in the United States. For aspiring leaders eager to make their mark in defense, policy, AI, cybersecurity, or emerging technology, this fellowship could be the ideal first step.
